title: Efficient Routing Protocol for Ad Hoc Transport Networks
author: Harish Chandra Maurya
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
ABSTRACT    
  An Automotive Ad Hoc Network (VANET) is a special type of Intelligent Transportation System (ITS). Routing protocol development in VANET is an important and necessary issue to support intelligent ITS. The key differences between VANETs and MANETs are their specialized mobility patterns and rapidly changing topologies. Existing MANET routing protocols are not suitable for VANETs. This document proposes SD-AOMDV as a VANET routing protocol. SD-AOMD enhances AOMDV, the most important on-demand multipath routing protocol, to fit the characteristics of VANETs.  
  SD-AOMDV adds mobility parameters such as hop speed and direction as a new AOMDV routing metric for next hop selection in the path discovery phase. Simulation results show that SD-AOMDV provides the best performance.
steps_raw:
ABSTRACT    
  An Automotive Ad Hoc Network (VANET) is a special type of Intelligent Transportation System (ITS). Routing protocol development in VANET is an important and necessary issue to support intelligent ITS. The key differences between VANETs and MANETs are their specialized mobility patterns and rapidly changing topologies. Existing MANET routing protocols are not suitable for VANETs. This document proposes SD-AOMDV as a VANET routing protocol. SD-AOMD enhances AOMDV, the most important on-demand multipath routing protocol, to fit the characteristics of VANETs.  
  SD-AOMDV adds mobility parameters such as hop speed and direction as a new AOMDV routing metric for next hop selection in the path discovery phase. Simulation results show that SD-AOMDV provides the best performance.
keywords: Intelligent Transport System, MANET, AOMDV, VANET, SD-AOMDV
